tasks.title: Implement grid model and game tick system
tasks.description: Create the foundational grid system and constant-rate tick timer that drives all game logic
tasks.details:
Create a Grid autoload singleton (res://grid.gd) with constants for grid dimensions (e.g., 20x15 cells), cell size (e.g., 32 pixels), and helper functions to convert between grid coordinates (Vector2i) and world positions (Vector2). In Main.tscn, add a Timer node configured for one-shot=false with a fixed wait_time (e.g., 0.15 seconds for ~6-7 moves/second). Connect the Timer's timeout signal to a _on_tick() function in the main script. Pseudo-code:

```gdscript
# grid.gd (autoload)
extends Node
const GRID_WIDTH: int = 20
const GRID_HEIGHT: int = 15
const CELL_SIZE: int = 32

func grid_to_world(grid_pos: Vector2i) -> Vector2:
    return Vector2(grid_pos.x * CELL_SIZE, grid_pos.y * CELL_SIZE)

func world_to_grid(world_pos: Vector2) -> Vector2i:
    return Vector2i(int(world_pos.x / CELL_SIZE), int(world_pos.y / CELL_SIZE))

func is_in_bounds(grid_pos: Vector2i) -> bool:
    return grid_pos.x >= 0 and grid_pos.x < GRID_WIDTH and grid_pos.y >= 0 and grid_pos.y < GRID_HEIGHT
```

```gdscript
# main.gd
extends Node2D

func _ready() -> void:
    $TickTimer.start()

func _on_tick() -> void:
    # Future: advance snake, check collisions
    pass
```

Test strategy: Create test/grid_test.gd to verify grid_to_world, world_to_grid, and is_in_bounds conversions.

tasks.title: Render arena floor and boundary walls
tasks.description: Display the playfield floor tiles and deadly wall tiles to establish the visible play area

tasks.title: Implement snake data structure and movement logic
tasks.description: Create the snake as an ordered array of grid cells that advances one cell per tick in the current direction

tasks.title: Implement arrow-key input with no-reverse constraint
tasks.description: Handle arrow key input to change snake direction, preventing 180-degree turns into the neck

tasks.title: Render snake head and body segments
tasks.description: Visually display the snake using player_head.png for the head and player_body.png for each body segment

tasks.title: Implement food spawn and respawn logic
tasks.description: Spawn food at a random empty grid cell and respawn to a new random empty cell when eaten

tasks.title: Render food sprite and play eat sound
tasks.description: Display the food using food.png and play eat_food.wav when the snake eats it
tasks.details:
In food.gd's _ready(), create a Sprite2D child with texture = preload('res://sprites/food.png'), texture_filter = TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST. Add an AudioStreamPlayer child with stream = preload('res://audio/eat_food.wav'). Add a play_eat_sound() method that calls audio_player.play(). In main.gd, when food is eaten (snake head == food position), call food.play_eat_sound() before respawning. Pseudo-code:

```gdscript
# food.gd
var sprite: Sprite2D
var audio_player: AudioStreamPlayer

func _ready() -> void:
    sprite = Sprite2D.new()
    sprite.texture = preload('res://sprites/food.png')
    sprite.texture_filter = CanvasItem.TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
    add_child(sprite)
    
    audio_player = AudioStreamPlayer.new()
    audio_player.stream = preload('res://audio/eat_food.wav')
    add_child(audio_player)

func play_eat_sound() -> void:
    audio_player.play()
```

```gdscript
# main.gd _on_tick()
if snake.get_head() == food.grid_pos:
    food.play_eat_sound()
    snake.grow()
    score += 1
    food.spawn(snake.body)
```

No unit tests required; verify audio plays on food consumption.

tasks.title: Implement collision detection and game-over state
tasks.description: Detect wall and self-collision to transition to game-over state and play death sound
tasks.details:
In main.gd, add a game_state enum (PLAYING, GAME_OVER). On each tick after snake.move(), check if snake head is out of bounds (!Grid.is_in_bounds(snake.get_head())) or if snake.check_self_collision() returns true. If either is true, transition to GAME_OVER state. Add an AudioStreamPlayer node to Main.tscn with stream = preload('res://audio/death.wav'). On collision, stop the tick timer, play death sound, and set game_state. Pseudo-code:

```gdscript
enum GameState { PLAYING, GAME_OVER }
var game_state: GameState = GameState.PLAYING

func _on_tick() -> void:
    if game_state != GameState.PLAYING:
        return
    
    snake.move()
    
    if not Grid.is_in_bounds(snake.get_head()) or snake.check_self_collision():
        _game_over()
        return
    
    # Check food collision...

func _game_over() -> void:
    game_state = GameState.GAME_OVER
    $TickTimer.stop()
    $DeathSound.play()
```

Test strategy: Create test/main_test.gd to verify collision detection triggers game-over state correctly for wall and self-collision scenarios.

tasks.title: Implement score tracking and HUD display
tasks.description: Track the player's score and display it in a minimal HUD
tasks.details:
In Main.tscn, add a CanvasLayer node named 'HUD' with a Label child positioned at the top-left (e.g., offset_left=10, offset_top=10). In main.gd, add a score: int variable initialized to 0. Each time food is eaten (task 7), increment score. Update the HUD label text with the current score. Pseudo-code:

```gdscript
var score: int = 0

func _ready() -> void:
    _update_score_display()

func _update_score_display() -> void:
    $HUD/ScoreLabel.text = "Score: %d" % score

func _on_tick() -> void:
    # ... existing tick logic
    if snake.get_head() == food.grid_pos:
        food.play_eat_sound()
        snake.grow()
        score += 1
        _update_score_display()
        food.spawn(snake.body)
```

No unit tests required; verify score increments and displays correctly during gameplay.

tasks.title: Implement game-over UI and restart functionality
tasks.description: Display a game-over prompt and allow the player to restart the game
tasks.details:
In Main.tscn's HUD CanvasLayer, add a ColorRect or Panel node covering the screen (initially hidden) with a Label child displaying 'Game Over! Press R to Restart'. In main.gd, when game_state becomes GAME_OVER, show this overlay. Listen for 'R' key press (or a custom restart action) in _input(); when detected and in GAME_OVER state, call a _restart_game() method that resets score to 0, recreates the snake at starting position/length, respawns food, sets game_state to PLAYING, hides the game-over overlay, and restarts the tick timer. Pseudo-code:

```gdscript
func _game_over() -> void:
    game_state = GameState.GAME_OVER
    $TickTimer.stop()
    $DeathSound.play()
    $HUD/GameOverPanel.visible = true

func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
    if game_state == GameState.GAME_OVER and event.is_action_pressed("ui_accept"):
        _restart_game()

func _restart_game() -> void:
    score = 0
    _update_score_display()
    snake.queue_free()
    snake = Snake.new()
    add_child(snake)
    food.spawn(snake.body)
    game_state = GameState.PLAYING
    $HUD/GameOverPanel.visible = false
    $TickTimer.start()
```

Test strategy: Create test/main_test.gd to verify restart resets score, snake, and game state correctly. Verify manually that pressing R restarts the game.
